name: Petra
description: Known as the Rose City, Petra is a UNESCO World Heritage Site carved into pink sandstone cliffs by the Nabataeans over 2,000 years ago. It is one of the New Seven Wonders of the World and Jordan's most visited attraction.

name: Wadi Rum
description: Also called the Valley of the Moon, Wadi Rum is a stunning desert landscape of towering sandstone mountains, natural arches, and ancient petroglyphs. Popular for camping, camel rides, and jeep tours.

name: Dead Sea
description: The lowest point on Earth at 430 meters below sea level. Famous for its extremely salty water where visitors float effortlessly, and its mineral-rich mud believed to have therapeutic properties.

name: Jerash
description: One of the best-preserved Roman provincial cities in the world. Jerash features colonnaded streets, grand temples, theaters, and public squares dating back over 2,000 years of continuous habitation.

name: Amman Citadel
description: Perched atop Jebel al-Qala'a in downtown Amman, the Citadel showcases ruins from Roman, Byzantine, and Umayyad periods including the Temple of Hercules and the Umayyad Palace.

name: Aqaba
description: Jordan's only coastal city on the Red Sea. Known for world-class diving and snorkeling, coral reefs, and warm waters year-round. A resort destination where desert meets the sea.

name: Ajloun Castle
description: Built by Saladin's general in 1184 AD to control the iron mines and protect trade routes. This Islamic castle offers panoramic views of the Jordan Valley and is a fine example of Ayyubid military architecture.

name: Madaba
description: Known as the City of Mosaics, Madaba is home to the famous 6th-century Mosaic Map of the Holy Land in the Greek Orthodox Church of St. George. The town has a rich tradition of Byzantine and Umayyad mosaics.

name: Mount Nebo
description: The mountain where Moses is believed to have seen the Promised Land before he died. On clear days, you can see the Dead Sea, Jordan Valley, Jericho, and the distant hills of Jerusalem from the summit.

name: Dana Nature Reserve
description: Jordan's largest nature reserve spanning 320 square kilometers from the Rift Valley to sandstone cliffs. Home to rare wildlife including the Nubian ibex, and offers spectacular hiking trails through varied ecosystems.

name: Karak Castle
description: A massive Crusader castle built in 1142 AD, perched on a hilltop overlooking the ancient King's Highway. It was a strategic stronghold during the Crusades and witnessed battles between Crusaders and Saladin's forces.

name: Roman Theater of Amman
description: A beautifully preserved 6,000-seat Roman theater built during the reign of Antoninus Pius. Carved into a hillside in downtown Amman, it still hosts cultural events and offers stunning views of the old city.

name: Umm Qais
description: Ancient Gadara of the Decapolis, Umm Qais overlooks the Sea of Galilee, the Golan Heights, and the Yarmouk River gorge. Features a black basalt theater, colonnaded streets, and stunning panoramic views.
